records.ety: [F. concours, L. concursus, fr. concurrere to run together. See Concur.]

defs: A moving, flowing, or running together; confluence.
quotes: The good frame of the universe was not the product of chance or fortuitous concourse of particles of matter.

defs: An assembly; a gathering formed by a voluntary or spontaneous moving and meeting in one place.
quotes: Amidst the concourse were to be seen the noble ladies of Milan, in gay, fantastic cars, shining in silk brocade.

defs: The place or point of meeting or junction of two bodies.
quotes: The drop will begin to move toward the concourse of the glasses.

defs: An open space where several roads or paths meet; esp. an open space in a park where several roads meet.

quotes: The divine providence is wont to afford its concourse to such proceeding.
